failure_modes.text: Vivid rehearsal can be mistaken for evidence, making confidence rise faster than the quality of the assumptions or data.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when vivid rehearsal is mistaken for evidence
failure_modes.mitigation: Separate vividness from evidence: after any mental rehearsal, list the empirical inputs it actually consumed and flag any confidence that exceeds the data.

failure_modes.text: Teams can keep simulating the preferred storyline while skipping ugly cases, tail risks, and disconfirming scenarios.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when teams keep simulating their preferred storyline
failure_modes.mitigation: Mandate at least one ugly-case simulation for every success-path rehearsal; require the team to simulate a failure scenario with the same level of detail.

failure_modes.text: Simulations can reinforce model-defending behavior when they are run mainly to confirm an existing leadership hypothesis.
failure_modes.mitigation: Assign a red-team member whose sole job is to run the simulation against the opposite hypothesis, not to confirm leadership's preferred story.

premortem_questions.text: What ugly case, hostile response, or edge condition would actually change this plan if it showed up in the simulation?
premortem_questions.source_quote: ugly cases, tail risks, and disconfirming scenarios

premortem_questions.text: What threshold, contingency, or trigger are we tying this simulation to so it is not just imaginative theater?
premortem_questions.source_quote: tied to concrete thresholds, pre-mortems, or contingency plans

premortem_questions.text: Which assumption in the simulation is least grounded and most capable of flipping the recommendation?

heuristics.text: Use simulation to compare multiple plausible futures, not to glorify the favorite plan.
heuristics.source_quote: multiple futures can be compared explicitly

heuristics.text: Pair the rehearsal with premortems, contingencies, and edge-case stress checks so it changes behavior rather than just confidence.
Treat simulation as the next best thing to experience, not as a substitute for reality.
heuristics.source_quote: not as good as actually doing something, but it is the next best thing

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on scenario rehearsal, ugly-case coverage, and keeping simulation from becoming confidence theater.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader role-play tooling sprawl because the raw source is stronger on assumption-testing and contingency discipline.
open_questions: The first premortem item compresses several source warnings about skipping ugly cases so the chunk remains concise.

allies: The raw ally surface is strongest on premortem, decision-trees, and systems-thinking because simulation is most useful when it drives explicit scenario comparison and contingency design.
antagonists: The raw antagonist pressure is strongest on confirmation-bias, optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy, and commitment-bias because each can corrupt what gets simulated.
structured_tensions: The source has explicit structured tensions with confirmation-bias, optimism-bias-and-planning-fallacy, and commitment-bias that cleanly fit the current target-id surface.
